name: Credit Card Management Services, Inc.
description_short: Credit Card Management Services is a Dallas, TX-based credit repair and financial consulting firm. 4.9 Google rating from 4,700+ reviews. Offers credit repair, credit counseling, and financial consulting services.
description_long:
Credit Card Management Services (CCMS) is a credit repair and financial consulting firm based in the Dallas-Fort Worth metroplex. The company specializes in helping consumers improve their credit scores by disputing inaccurate, unverifiable, or outdated negative items on credit reports with all three major bureaus — Equifax, Experian, and TransUnion. With over 4,700 Google reviews at a 4.9-star rating, CCMS has built a substantial local reputation in the DFW area, suggesting consistent client satisfaction and likely a strong referral-based business model.

CCMS offers credit repair services that include comprehensive credit report analysis, identification of disputable items, preparation and filing of dispute letters with credit bureaus and original creditors, debt validation requests, and ongoing monitoring of dispute progress. The financial consulting component suggests the firm also works with clients on broader financial planning — budgeting, debt management strategy, and credit-building plans beyond just dispute-based repair. This holistic approach can be more effective than firms that focus exclusively on deletion tactics.

As with all credit repair companies, consumers should understand the legal framework. The Credit Repair Organizations Act (CROA) prohibits upfront fees before services are rendered, requires written contracts, and provides a three-day cancellation right. No credit repair company can legally guarantee removal of accurate negative information or promise specific score improvements. The company does not have BBB accreditation and limited national visibility, which is typical for locally-focused firms. Consumers should request detailed pricing, a written contract, and references before enrolling.

In the broader ecosystem of credit repair services, consumers have multiple paths to improving their credit. Professional credit repair companies can dispute inaccurate items with all three bureaus, while credit monitoring services provide ongoing alerts about changes to your reports. For those building credit from scratch, secured credit cards and credit builder loans offer structured approaches. Consumers dealing with overwhelming debt may benefit from debt consolidation loans to simplify payments, or credit counseling through nonprofit agencies for personalized budgeting guidance. Tools like a credit score simulator help project the impact of different decisions on scores. Consumers who successfully repair their credit often find better rates on installment loans, secured credit cards, and other financial products.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

tiers.name: Debt Management Plan
features: Late fee and over-limit fee elimination

certifications: No BBB accreditation or rating found

pros: Non-profit organization — fee structure is regulated and mission-driven rather than profit-motivated
Outstanding consumer reputation: 4.9/5 stars from over 4,700 Google reviews, an unusually high volume for a financial services firm
Negotiates directly with major credit card issuers to reduce interest rates on enrolled accounts
Single monthly payment consolidates multiple creditor obligations, simplifying repayment
Offers federally required bankruptcy counseling and debtor education certificates
National service availability — not limited to Florida residents despite West Palm Beach headquarters
cons: Enrollment in a Debt Management Plan typically requires closing participating credit card accounts, which can temporarily harm your credit score
DMPs address unsecured debt only — no help for mortgages, car loans, or federal student loans
Repayment timelines of three to five years require sustained financial discipline; a single missed payment can cause creditors to revoke negotiated rate reductions
Credit counseling is not debt settlement — balances are repaid in full, which may not be viable for consumers with very high debt-to-income ratios
Monthly program fees, while typically low for non-profits, vary by state and should be confirmed before enrollment
best_for: Dallas-Fort Worth area residents who want a local credit repair provider with in-person consultation availability
Consumers with inaccurate negative items on their credit reports who want professional dispute assistance
Individuals preparing for home purchases or auto financing who need credit score improvement
People who prefer a smaller, locally-focused credit repair firm over national chains
diagnosis: CCMS is a locally-focused Dallas credit repair firm with an impressive 4.9 Google rating from 4,700+ reviews — suggesting genuine client satisfaction. The local presence offers in-person consultation advantages over national remote-only firms. However, the lack of BBB accreditation, limited online pricing transparency, and minimal national visibility are typical limitations of regional credit repair providers. Consumers should compare pricing against national providers like The Credit People ($79/mo), Lexington Law, or Sky Blue Credit before committing. DIY-capable consumers can dispute items for free through AnnualCreditReport.com.
services: Debt Management Plans (DMPs) with creditor-negotiated interest rate reductions
Single consolidated monthly payment processing to multiple creditors
Bankruptcy debtor education (post-filing financial management courses)
Creditor negotiation and account hardship program enrollment
Debt-to-income analysis and repayment scenario modeling
Ongoing client support and account monitoring throughout DMP enrollment
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
